data.title: Industrial control system loop check
data.solution:
zero = int(input())
span = int(input())
mv = int(input())

output = (((mv - zero) / (span - zero)) * 16) + 4
print(f"{output:.2f} mA")
data.statement: An instrument technician will be expected to know the current in a control loop if they are given  the range details, along with the reading of the measured variable. You must determine the current in each circuit, given the above information.
data.stubGenerator:
read zero:int

read mv:int
write output: mA
data.inputDescription: You are given the range of the control loop for zero, [[zero]] and the top of the range, [[span]]. You are then given the measured variable, [[mv]]. Please note that the zero point may be zero, negative or elevated.
data.outputDescription:
You must determine what the input current to the control system is in milliamps (mA). The [[output]] from the transmitter in the control loop will be between 4 mA, representing the bottom of the range, and 20 mA, representing the very top of the range. This mA output must be given to 2 decimal places. 

The control systems engineer requires you to calculate the loop current for each circuit as soon as possible.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

An instrument technician will be expected to know the current in a control loop if they are given the range details, along with the reading of the measured variable. You must determine the current in each circuit, given the above information.
Input
You are given the range of the control loop for zero, zero and the top of the range, span. You are then given the measured variable, mv. Please note that the zero point may be zero, negative or elevated.
Output
You must determine what the input current to the control system is in milliamps (mA). The output from the transmitter in the control loop will be between 4 mA, representing the bottom of the range, and 20 mA, representing the very top of the range. This mA output must be given to 2 decimal places.

The control systems engineer requires you to calculate the loop current for each circuit as soon as possible.
Constraints
-1000 <= zero <= 1000
0.1 <= span <=10000
-1000 <= mv <=10000
4.00 mA <= output <= 20.00 mA
Example
Input
0
10
5
Output
12.00 mA
